creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text: These days, BIO_DELETE is almost always a win. Writing zeros is a big lose often (though if the lower-layers implement a special WRITE ZEROS command, there might be some cases where that's useful: we currently do not). I doubt BOTH are ever anything but extra work. I would argue, however, that we should think carefully here. The difference between DSM Deallocate and WRITE ZEROS + Deallocate flag (for those nvme drives that support it) is very small (trimmed blocks can return a variety of things depending: 0, ff or last written data)...
